title: AFI Enrichment Category Result — pattern v1 (afi.enrichment.pattern.v1)
description: The canonical output contract for the 'pattern' open analysis lane: the ONE resolved result a pattern provider/plugin contributes to the scorer-facing join (afi.enrichment.pattern.v1). A provider-produced pattern result MUST pass this validation at the provider-adapter edge BEFORE it reaches scoring (PBF-GOV D-PBF-8); a malformed result never reaches the scorer. The category marker MUST be 'pattern' (the join classifies one result per category by this marker). The result is provider-neutral and market-neutral: it carries normalized, bounded pattern OBSERVATIONS (motifs, anomaly/discord detections, change-point/regime boundaries, support/resistance pivots, and an OPTIONAL bounded candlestick/structure observation block per FLPR-GOV D-FLPR-3), each tied to deterministic input positions (and, when the analyzed series declared epoch-millisecond timestamps, an optional domain timestamp). It carries no raw matrix profile, no complete time series, no raw library output, no price/level values (levels are normalized to the analyzed series range), no scoring/UWR/rank/reward, no provider identity, and no credential material.
$comment: BOUNDARY: category-result payload contract — this validates a runtime category output, not a stored artifact, so it carries no 'schema' const field; the discriminator is the 'category' marker. It is referenced by the analysis-plugin outputSchemaRef 'afi.enrichment.pattern.v1'. additionalProperties:false at every object level leaves no room for a raw provider payload, a credential-like field, a params bag, or an Evidence provenance stamp to ride in the result. Observations are explicit typed enums/fields — never an arbitrary object.
x-afiPartOf: AFI Provider Adapter & BYOK Foundations v0.1 (PBF-GOV)
x-afiDoctrineRefs: afi-governance/decisions/provider-byok-foundations-v0.1 (PBF-GOV) D-PBF-2/D-PBF-8 — one resolved result per category; provider output passes canonical category validation before scoring

afi-governance/decisions/factory-configurable-pipelines-v1 (FCP-GOV) D-FCP-1/D-FCP-5 — the 'pattern' open analysis lane; conforming providers/plugins are administrative registry updates, not per-participant governance

afi-governance/decisions/five-lane-provider-runtime-v0.1 (FLPR-GOV) D-FLPR-3 — the bounded additive optional 'candlestick' observation block (name enum + integer confidence + structure flags), authorized so the score-bearing pattern observation can ride the governed contract

category.description: Category marker. MUST be 'pattern' — the deterministic one-per-category join classifies contributions by this marker.

series.description: Non-secret identity and shape of the analyzed series. Provides the deterministic frame every observation index/timestamp refers to. Carries no series values (normalized results only, never the raw time series).

seriesId.description: Provider-neutral, non-secret identity of the analyzed series (e.g. an opaque signal-derived series id). Never a URL, credential, or raw provider payload.

length.description: Number of ordered observations analyzed. Every observation index is a zero-based position in [0, length).

indexBasis.description: How observation positions/timestamps are expressed: 'position' = zero-based ordinal only; 'epochMs' = ordinal positions PLUS an optional per-observation epoch-millisecond timestamp.

motifs.description: Bounded, normalized motif (repeated-subsequence) observations from matrix-profile motif discovery. Empty when none are found.

windowSize.description: Subsequence length the motif was found over.

index.description: Zero-based start position of the motif subsequence.

neighborIndex.description: Zero-based start position of the nearest matching subsequence.

similarity.description: Normalized similarity in [0,1] (1 = identical), derived from the z-normalized matrix-profile distance divided by its theoretical maximum. Market-neutral.

timestamp.description: OPTIONAL epoch-millisecond domain timestamp of the motif start; present only when series.indexBasis is 'epochMs'.

discords.description: Bounded, normalized anomaly (discord) observations from matrix-profile discord detection. Empty when none are found.

windowSize.description: Subsequence length the discord was evaluated over.

index.description: Zero-based start position of the anomalous subsequence.

anomalyScore.description: Normalized anomaly strength in [0,1] (1 = most anomalous), derived from the z-normalized matrix-profile distance divided by its theoretical maximum. Market-neutral.

timestamp.description: OPTIONAL epoch-millisecond domain timestamp of the discord start; present only when series.indexBasis is 'epochMs'.

changePoints.description: Bounded, normalized change-point / regime-boundary observations from deterministic change-point detection. Empty when none are found.

index.description: Zero-based position of the detected regime boundary (start of the new segment).

magnitude.description: Normalized magnitude of the level shift at the boundary in [0,1], the absolute change in adjacent-segment means divided by the analyzed series range. Market-neutral.

timestamp.description: OPTIONAL epoch-millisecond domain timestamp of the boundary; present only when series.indexBasis is 'epochMs'.

pivots.description: Bounded, normalized support/resistance pivot observations from deterministic peak detection. Empty when none are found.

kind.description: Whether the pivot is a local trough (support) or local peak (resistance).

level.description: Normalized position of the pivot value within the analyzed series range in [0,1] (0 = series minimum, 1 = series maximum). Market-neutral — carries no raw price.

prominence.description: Normalized topographic prominence of the pivot in [0,1], the raw prominence divided by the analyzed series range. Market-neutral.

timestamp.description: OPTIONAL epoch-millisecond domain timestamp of the pivot; present only when series.indexBasis is 'epochMs'.

candlestick.description: OPTIONAL bounded candlestick/structure observation (FLPR-GOV D-FLPR-3): the single strongest detected named candlestick pattern plus optional structure context. Vendor-neutral observation vocabulary only — a categorical name from a closed enum and an integer confidence grade; never a scoring instruction, price, or raw series value. Absent when no named pattern is detected.

patternName.description: The detected named candlestick pattern (closed vendor-neutral vocabulary).

patternConfidence.description: Integer confidence grade of the detection in [0,100]. A categorical observation grade — deliberately NOT normalized to [0,1] like the kernel observation scores.

flags.description: OPTIONAL per-pattern detection booleans for the four named patterns.

structureBias.description: OPTIONAL detected market-structure bias over the analyzed window.

trendPullbackConfirmed.description: OPTIONAL deterministic pullback-confirmation observation over the analyzed window.
